Assign cell to groups (eg decide on double pos)

This is done in line 145-149

Path: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Data\Organoids\Scripts\384_Boxplots_Cellcounts_ImageByImage.R

proportion table with these ratios per patient per image:

Ratios:

CK7positive_allcells_ratio, CK7alive_allCK7_ratio, CK7alive_allcells_ratio, CD45positive_allcells_ratio, CD45alive_allCD45_ratio, CD45alive_allcells_ratio

Filter outliers

Removing images that contain no CK7+, CD45+, less than 5 cells in total, or containing cell population more than 3 SD above the mean

Mean and median ratio per all the images of the same condition per patient

Normalize by dividing the mean per condition by the mean of DMSO 2 (control sample because drugs were dissolved in DMSO 2)

And median

Generate hmaps

Select column with logchange values of mean ratios per condition

Change layout of dataset

log 2fc instead of fc log(ratios + 1, 2)

+1 prevents -inf values that are represented as gray dots in heatmap

log2(0) is undefined

Decided not to transform data and keep gray dots

median instead of mean

Automate truncating ratio names in heatmaps !

Max and min for pvalue for legend

Run over all patients (standardize folder naming) !!

One heatmap with all drugs and patients per ratio

Make a presentation describing the changes made and why?

How did we handle double positives

What ratios were used

Other ratios that could calculated

How did we determine outliers

Other ways to decide outliers, should we filter outliers at all?

Treating images as technical replicates

Given they are not clustering by well

Normalization

Calculating statistics (Wilcoxon)

6.6.23

In order to automatize drug treatment plotting:

Using consistent naming for data files for treatment layout (ex: S141_pOval instead of S141 for treatment layout)

Each sample folder should contain file titled csvs with the raw data files

Question answered by Jie

Why are we using the Integrated Intensity for CK7 and Mean Intensity for CD45?

To distinguish between the intensity of these two cell populations the integrated intensity, which accounts for the size of the cell, is recorded for the tumor cells. Since tumor cells are larger the intensity values for the tumor cells are also greater allowing for gating by intensity

Most of the double positives in S141 were dead and the double positives cells (CK7+ and CD45+) were not visible in the images. How do we approach categorizing these cells?

Since it is not clear whether the CK7 or CD45 cells have become apoptotic, we have decided to include double positives in the all cell count, but we will categorize them as double negatives

In S141, less than 2% of all cells were double positive

What percentage of the CK7+ and CD45+ cells are double positives?

7.6.23

Question for Matilda

S141: When merging the IF data file with the Treatment_layout csv we noticed that 4 wells are present in the IF data file but not in the treatment layout file (D05, H16, O12, O22). These wells we assumed were not treated with drugs. But there are wells in the treatment layout file that are not in the data file (E12, G12, H12, I12, K12, M12). Do you have an idea about why this is?

Check the raw files (from CellProfiler) and images

From the images it looks like the signal intensity is low, cells are not segmented, and therefore are not in data file (E12, K12, M12)

Dead cells no DAPI signal

Cells not segmented but sample is in data file?! (G12 in 141_2ndhalfE, but this is the file outputted by CellProfiler)

Some images do not exist, perhaps the microscope skipped over these wells (H12, I12)

To do:

-fix legend scale so 0 is white

-make heatmap for LD percentages

-automate combined heatmap script

10.7.23 -18.7.23

Write script for samples plated on slides

Samples: S071_pOme, S085_pOme, S102_pOme (96w), S119_pOme, S114_pOme (Cell Profiler analysis incomplete), S038_rAdn (PARPi resistant), S041_rOva (PARPi resistant), S045_rOva (PARPi resistant)

Path to csvs: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Data\Organoids\Drug treatment_slides_96wells

Path to Scripts: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Data\Organoids\Scripts\Modified Scripts (AS)\Slide_96w_exceptions

Path to Inventory with intensity threshold values: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Data\Organoids\Scripts\Sample_Inventory_slides_96w_treatment.xlsx

Path to Plots: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Data\Organoids\AS_Plots\Slide_96 well

Opted to plot these sample manually, so each sample has its own script given the subtle differences in csvs formatting.

Ashwini and I noticed inconsistencies between Elina and our plots for PARPi resistant samples. We went back to the cell profiler pipeline.

CK7 nuclei intensity threshold is 40 versus 20 (AN)

However changing the threshold from 20 to 40 in the most recent scripts does not drastically change the plots (for example with S041 only 7 images were excluded when the higher threshold was applied

Elina plotted fold change of CK7 cell counts, which was calculated by dividing the CK7 counts per images by the average DMSO 2 CK7 cell counts. I plotted this value as well, however these two modifications do not explain the inconsistencies between plots

Questions for Jie and Matilda

In cell profiler, how was the CK7 threshold of 40 determined?

Cells (objects) were either assigned as a cancer or noncancer cell depending on the Children_Nuclei_cancer_Count value

Q: why was the nuclei intensity used for CK7 marker over cell intensity?

Children_Cell_Cancer_Count and Children_Nuclei_cancer_Count do not contain the same values, so the two are not interchangeable

However, the data in the Cell column can not be plotted

How was the Nuclei column generated?

19.7.23

Automate boxplot and heatmap script per sample using the threshold values from Sample Inventory_384

Scanner with Ashwini

Meeting with Jie and Matilda about slide_96w samples (did not shed much light on Elina’s analysis method. It is possible than she filtered out data points in excel and plotted those results in R)

The trends roughly hold except Adavosertib-WEE1i 10 is not longer an effective combination therapy

Edited the derandomization script from Matilda

25.7.23

Plotted heatmap per sample with all ratios and drug combinations

In the future, Ashwini wants to plot all samples together per drug combination per ratio (one for CK7ratio and another CD45ratio)

In the meantime

Dead cell marker column name issue for 141

And figure out why there are NA values in 141 plots

Legend range (is there a way to automate this?)

Order for the drug combinations (can we automate)

Add the for loop back

For loop is glitching, double check this…

Read about project and start writing an outline

Ask Annina for dates around mid august for a meeting

Hello Pirkko,

I’m Aditi Sirsikar, a master’s student in Anniina Farkkila’s lab. We have HGSC patient-derived immunocompetent cultures in a humanized matrix. We would like to run scRNA sequencing on these samples.

Describe how the samples have been stored. These are the control samples, they are not treated with any drugs only DMSO 2 and the purpose of the experiments is to evaluate whether the cultured sample recapitulates main gene expression signatures as the original (ex. Vivo = out of the living) parental tumor and to tease out pathways up and down regulated in the tumor facilitating growth of the tumor to provide an explanation for the drug responses plotted earlier.

We have a few questions about potential interference from foreign rna from the gel matrix derived from human tissue (pooled omentum tissue from multiple patient samples)

Another question: how do you recommend separating the clumps of cells (mechanically using a strainer?)

----Decided that we will run a short cycif experiment instead of single cell sequencing

4.9.23

Plan for the week:

Document the work you have done

Explain each set of graphs

Add ReadMe text files for each folder

Clean the scripts make them readable and easy to edit

Standard naming of drugs when preparing treatment layout excel, otherwise automation is difficult

- Plot ATR1 uM and other drugs in combination as single agents, and combination of ATR1+ others for ED75-DMSO and Toxicity plot (CK7 dead/all cells - cd45 dead/all cells).

- Legend colors: red for more ck7 death

- Plot ED50-DMSO for Pembro and PARPi single agent and toxicity plot for the same

- Separate the STING plot into two: One plot containing samples S141, S147, S156 other with rest of the samples. For this also make ED75-DMSO and killing efficiency plot

- For all the plots legend can be red-white-blue
